Check the HV21 (liabilities) section. For each of these item numbers, does it appear as a **bold section header** in the document?

- Item 221 (Spareinlagen / Savings deposits - sub-item of 222)
- Item 270 (Rückstellungen / Provisions for liabilities and charges)

Also: In the HV21 section, do Items 280, 290, 300, 310, 320 each appear as separate bold headers? Or are some of them combined/merged?

And: Is there any Item 031 or Item 021 anywhere in the document (HV11 section)?

Return a simple YES/NO for each item, with page number if possible.

input.prompt:
This is a German banking statistics document. I need detailed information about specific item numbers. Please search the entire document carefully and answer:

1. In the HV21 section (liabilities/Passivseite): Does Item 221 (Spareinlagen / Savings deposits) appear as a bold section header on its own line?
2. In the HV21 section: Does Item 270 (Rückstellungen / Provisions for liabilities and charges) appear as a bold section header on its own line?
3. In the HV21 section: Do Items 280, 290, 300, 310, 320 each appear as separate bold headers, or are some combined/merged?
4. In the HV11 section (or anywhere in the document): Is there any Item 031? Is there any Item 021?

For each item, provide YES or NO, the exact text shown, and the page number if visible. Be as precise as possible about bold formatting and whether items are standalone headers vs sub-items.

Please provide the document content in plain text format, and I'll give you precise YES/NO answers with exact quotes (≤125 characters) and page references.

input.command:
python3 -c "
import subprocess
result = subprocess.run(['pdftotext', '-layout', '/home/perni/.claude/projects/-home-perni-clexp/cb4c6cb1-15c5-4745-a8f4-d21a078d2929/tool-results/webfetch-1774465671377-tc438q.pdf', '/tmp/bundesbank_output.txt'], capture_output=True, text=True)

print('Stdout:', result.stdout)
print('Stderr:', result.stderr)
"

Check extracted text size and preview

content.content:
6414 /tmp/bundesbank_output.txt
DEUTSCHE
BUNDESBANK

Banking statistics guidelines
January 2026




                 Monthly balance sheet statistics guidelines

                                Unofficial English translation

               Only the original German version is binding.




                                                                 37
 DEUTSCHE
BUNDESBANK

Banking statistics guidelines
January 2026




Guidelines on the monthly balance sheet statistics of monetary
financial institutions (MFIs) and non-MFI credit institutions

Data collection scope

The monthly balance sheet statistics (BISTA) represent the most comprehensive statistical survey
of the banking industry in Germany and are at the core of the banking statistics reporting system.
Data are collected on the assets and liabilities of domestic banks (MFIs) and non-MFI credit
institutions, for the part of the institution located in Germany (domestic part), based on their books
at the end of the month. MFIs report their balance sheet and additional annexes providing
analyticallyimportant breakdowns of balance sheet items by debtor and creditor type, term and
sector.
Pursuant to Section 4 of the German Financial and Internal Capital Adequacy Information
Regulation (Verordnung zur Einreichung von Finanz- und Risikotragfähigkeitsinformationen,
FinaRisikoV), data reported to BISTA also serve as prudential financial information (statement of
assets and liabilities).

An overview of the reporting templates to be submitted by reporting agents (1. banks (MFIs)
(including electronic money institutions (MFIs)), 2. building and loan associations (MFIs) and 3.
non-MFI credit institutions) can be found in the section “List of reports from banks (MFIs) and non-
MFI credit institutions for the monthly balance sheet statistics”.

See “List of reports from banks (MFIs) and non-MFI credit institutions for the monthly balance sheet statistics”.




                                                                                                                    38
     DEUTSCHE
    BUNDESBANK

    Banking statistics guidelines
    January 2026


    Guidelines on the individual items on the main templates
    ((HV1) and (HV2))                   1




    I. Assets (HV11 and HV12)

    Item 010 Cash in hand
             (Kassenbestand)

    Legal tender, including foreign banknotes and coins, postage stamps and court fee stamps are to be
    shown under this item. Legal tender denominated in Deutsche Mark or the legacy currencies of the
    other euro area Member States accepted in exchange for euro are also to be reported here.
    Commemorative coins bought at a price higher than the face value, gold coins (even if they are legal
    tender) and gold bullion are to be shown under item HV11/176 “Others”.

    Item 011 Domestic legal tender
             (Inländische gesetzliche Zahlungsmittel)

    Only euro banknotes and coins and legal tender denominated in Deutsche Mark or the legacy
    currencies of the other euro area Member States accepted in exchange for euro as reported under
    “cash in hand” are to be shown under this item.

    Item 020 Balances with central banks
             (Guthaben bei Zentralnotenbanken)

    This item may include only those balances that are readily available at all times, including foreign
    currency overnight balances with the central banks of the countries in which the reporting bank is
    established. Other balances, such as overnight balances held as part of the Bundesbank deposit
    facility, as well as claims on the Deutsche Bundesbank arising from sale and repurchase transactions
    and time deposits are to be shown under item HV11/061 “Loans and advances to banks (MFIs)”.

    Loans from central banks, e.g. overnight loans taken up through the Bundesbank marginal lending
    facility, or other loans and advances repayable on demand are not to be deducted from the balances
    but shown under item HV21/210 “Liabilities to banks (MFIs)”. Any credit balances recorded in the
    Bundesbank giro account are to be shown under item HV21/326 “Others”.

1111:    Item 221 Savings deposits
1389:Item 280 Subordinated liabilities
1432:Item 290 Capital represented by participation rights
1463:Item 300 Fund for general banking risks
1491:Item 310 Capital
1550:Item 320 Other liabilities

input.description: Broader search for item 270 and related terms

content.content:
1370:“Provisions for liabilities and charges”.
1374:Item 260 Provisions for liabilities and charges
1375:         (Rückstellungen)
4442:    HV.270/03 and H./280/03 are not to be specified in €1,000 but in the full euro value (rounded, ignoring
4560:waive until the allocation of contracts, are to be shown under item HV21/ 260 “Provisions for liabilities

Item 220 Liabilities to non-banks (non-MFIs)                      1



             (Verbindlichkeiten gegenüber Nichtbanken (Nicht-MFIs))

    All liabilities to domestic and foreign non-banks, other than securitised liabilities (item HV21/230) or
    subordinated liabilities (item HV21/280), are to be shown under this item. This also includes
    syndicated loans taken out (to be reported separately under item HV22/223).

    Item 221 Savings deposits
             (Spareinlagen)

    Only those funds without a specified maturity which satisfy the requirements of Section 21(4) of the
    German Regulation on the Accounting of Credit Institutions (Verordnung über die Rechnungslegung
    der Kreditinstitute und Finanzdienstleistungsinstitute) are to be shown as savings deposits. Section

1
    For building and loan associations: including deposits under savings and loan contracts placed by banks; see also “Supplementary guidelines...”
    pp. 116 f.


                                                                                                                                             56
 DEUTSCHE
BUNDESBANK

Banking statistics guidelines
January 2026
39(6) of the German Regulation on the Accounting of Credit Institutions (Verordnung über die
Rechnungslegung der Kreditinstitute und Finanzdienstleistungsinstitute) is to be applied. This also

January 2026
Value adjustments should generally be deducted from the relevant asset items. Notwithstanding this
and the disclosure requirements under commercial law, general value adjustments for latent
counterparty credit risk in lending business and individual country risk value adjustments should not
                                                              1



be deducted but instead shown under this item.
For provisions established for contingent liabilities and undrawn irrevocable credit commitments, see item HV21/260
“Provisions for liabilities and charges”.



Item 260 Provisions for liabilities and charges
         (Rückstellungen)

Provisions established for contingent liabilities and for undrawn irrevocable credit commitments
should also be shown under this item. This includes value adjustments deriving from recourse claims
arising from items HV21/341 to HV21/343. The same applies to value adjustments deriving from non-
capitalised assets which have been sold with an option to repurchase and in respect of which
repurchase commitments have been shown under item HV21/370. Notwithstanding Section 24 of the
German Accounting Regulation for Credit Institutions (Verordnung über die Rechnungslegung der
Kreditinstitute und Finanzdienstleistungsinstitute), contingent liabilities arising from the endorsement
of rediscounted bills (item HV21/341) should not be shown net of value adjustments.

Bonus payments due on the expiry of current saving contracts should also be shown under this item
even if premature withdrawal of amounts saved is not permitted under the terms of the contract.

Item 280 Subordinated liabilities
         (Nachrangige Verbindlichkeiten)

All liabilities – whether securitised or not – are to be shown under this item which, in the event of
winding-up or bankruptcy, may be repaid only after the claims of all other creditors have been met.
Repurchased non-negotiable own subordinated paper is to be deducted at the value at which it is
carried on the liability side.

Liabilities deriving from drawn, called or matured but not yet paid subordinated own debt securities
are still to be shown here.

Item 281 Subordinated negotiable debt securities
         (Nachrangig begebene börsenfähige Schuldverschreibungen)

Item 282 Subordinated non-negotiable debt securities
         (Nachrangig begebene nicht börsenfähige Schuldverschreibungen)
See “Guidelines on the individual items on the main template (HV1 and HV2)”, “II. Liabilities (HV21 and HV22)”, of which item
HV22/284.


Item 284 Subordinated non-negotiable debt securities; of which: denominated in euro
         (Nachrangig begebene nicht börsenfähige Schuldverschreibungen; darunter: auf Euro

61
 DEUTSCHE
BUNDESBANK

Banking statistics guidelines
January 2026
Item 285 Subordinated registered debt securities; of which: denominated in euro
         (Nachrangig begebene Namensschuldverschreibungen; darunter: auf Euro lautend)
See “Guidelines on the individual items on the main template (HV1 and HV2)”, “II. Liabilities (HV21 and HV22)”, item HV22/327.


Item 290 Capital represented by participation rights
         (Genussrechtskapital)

Capital represented by participation rights or comparable capital in the nature of equity is to be shown
here as soon as it has been received by the reporting institution and irrespective of whether the
participation rights are evidenced by securities or in some other form or take the shape of rights not
evidenced by certificates.

Capital not in the nature of equity is not to be shown here but, depending on its terms, under item
HV21/230 or under items HV21/210 or HV21/220 or, in the case of participation rights not evidenced
by securities and not repayable, under item HV21/326.

Liabilities deriving from drawn, called or matured but not yet paid own capital evidenced by certificates
are still to be shown here. Repurchased own participation rights are to be deducted.
See item HV/22.295 “Additional regulatory tier 1 instruments”.


Item 295 Additional regulatory tier 1 instruments
         (Instrumente des zusätzlichen aufsichtsrechtlichen Kernkapitals)

Reporting is based on the rules on single-entity financial statements contained in the German
Regulation on the Accounting of Credit Institutions (Verordnung über die Rechnungslegung der
Kreditinstitute) and in the German Commercial Code (Handelsgesetzbuch), provided no special
provisions were made in the following. If, based on the overall picture presented by the
circumstances, the additional regulatory tier 1 instruments predominantly have the attributes of
neither a participation right nor a subordinated liability (HV21/280), they should be recorded as capital
represented by participation rights in item HV21/290 and additionally in item HV22/295.
See item HV21/290 “Capital represented by participation rights”.



Item 300 Fund for general banking risks
         (Fonds für allgemeine Bankrisiken)

The “Fund for general banking risks” created pursuant to Section 340g of the German Commercial
Code (Handelsgesetzbuch) should be shown under this item.

Item 301 Amounts pursuant to Section 340e(4) of the German Commercial Code
         (Handelsgesetzbuch)
         (Beträge gemäß § 340e Abs. 4 HGB)

The amounts earmarked in the fund for general banking risks pursuant to Section 340g of the
German Commercial Code (Handelsgesetzbuch) that are attributable to the special item pursuant
to Section 340e(4) of the German Commercial Code (Handelsgesetzbuch) should be shown here.

Item 302 Other earmarked amounts
         (Sonstige zweckgebundene Beträge)
                                                                                                                          62
 DEUTSCHE
BUNDESBANK

Banking statistics guidelines
January 2026


The amounts earmarked in the fund for general banking risks pursuant to Section 340g of the German
Commercial Code (Handelsgesetzbuch) should be shown here. Amounts should be considered
earmarked if they may only be released for a specific, pre-defined intended purpose.

Item 310 Capital
         (Eigenkapital)

Capital items, including an increase or reduction in them, should be shown according to the principle
of reporting accounts as they stand and irrespective of formal intermediate steps (preparation and
adoption of annual financial statements) having been completed.

Any shortfall not covered by capital should also be shown here, preceded by a minus sign.
See “General guidelines”, III. “General reporting rules and other explanatory notes”, “Ledger-level principle”.



Item 311 Subscribed capital
         (Gezeichnetes Kapital)

All amounts which (irrespective of their precise designations) are deemed, in accordance with the
legal form of the bank concerned, to be capital subscribed by the shareholders (including silent
partners) or other proprietors should be shown here; capital contributed by silent partners,
endowment capital and the amounts paid up on members’ shares (including those of members who
are retiring from a cooperative society with effect from the reporting date) should also be included in
this item. Domestic branches of foreign banks should show the working capital made available to
them by their foreign head office and the operating surplus left to them for the purpose of
strengthening their own resources.

In the case of sole proprietorships and partnerships, private accounts, variable equity accounts and
similar accounts held by the proprietors or partners should be shown under this item only if they
exhibit the characteristics of capital; this applies even if the accounts concerned show a negative
balance. In the case of cooperative societies, the amounts actually paid up on members’ shares
should be shown.
For subscribed capital unpaid, see item HV11/150 with the same designation.
For the treatment of deposits by silent partners, see the notes on item HV21/290.


Item 312 Reserves
         (Rücklagen)

Capital and revenue reserves, including the reserve fund (Sicherheitsrücklagen) of savings banks
and the revenue reserves of credit cooperatives, should be shown under this item.
See items HV22/515, HV22/521.


Item 313 Net loss of the previous financial year and loss brought forward
         (Nettofehlbetrag des vorangegangenen Geschäftsjahres und Verlustvortrag)

If expenditure exceeded income in the previous financial year (net loss), the part of the balance of all
income and expenditure entries of the previous financial year which, as part of work on the annual
financial statements, has not yet been included in other items of the monthly balance sheet statistics
should be shown here.
                                                                                                                  63
 DEUTSCHE
BUNDESBANK

Banking statistics guidelines
January 2026

Any loss brought forward should also be recorded under this item.
See items HV11/175, HV21/325, HV21/300, HV21/311, HV21/312, HV22/339 and HV22/510.


Item 320 Other liabilities
         (Sonstige Passiva)

Only those liabilities which cannot or cannot yet be assigned to another item are to be shown under
this item. For details, see items HV21/321 to HV21/326.



Item 321 Interest accrued on zero coupon bonds
          (Aufgelaufene Zinsen auf Null-Kupon Anleihen)

    Of the securitised liabilities with a maturity of up to and including two years recorded under item

    For non-MFI credit institutions subject to minimum reserve requirements established in Germany or in another euro area

    (other than domestic banks (MFIs))”.


    XXXVI Changes resulting from valuation adjustments in the reporting month

    All changes in loan and securities portfolios resulting from valuation adjustments in the reporting
    month are to be shown in the following supplements (outflows are to be indicated by a minus sign):

    – A1B = Supplementary report to Annex A1
    – B1B = Supplementary report to Annex B1
    – B3B = Supplementary report to Annex B3
    – B4B = Supplementary report to Annex B4
    – B6B = Supplementary report to Annex B6

content.text:
Here are the answers to each question, based on direct extraction from the PDF text:

---

**Item 221 (Spareinlagen / Savings deposits) — bold section header in HV21?**
YES. It appears as a standalone item header: `Item 221 Savings deposits (Spareinlagen)` on approximately page 56. However, it is NOT a sub-item of Item 222 — the document structure is the opposite: 221 and 222 are both sub-items of Item 220 (Liabilities to non-banks). Item 221 precedes Item 222 ("Other liabilities to non-banks"), not the other way around.

**Item 270 (Rückstellungen / Provisions for liabilities and charges) — bold section header in HV21?**
NO. There is no `Item 270` in the document at all. The provisions item in HV21 is **Item 260** (`Provisions for liabilities and charges / Rückstellungen`). The number 270 appears only once in the entire document in a form-field reference (`HV.270/03`) in an annex about minimum reserve calculations — it is not a balance sheet line item header.

**Items 280, 290, 300, 310, 320 — each a separate bold header in HV21?**
YES to all five — they each appear as separate, standalone item headers:
- Item 280 — `Subordinated liabilities (Nachrangige Verbindlichkeiten)` (~page 61)
- Item 290 — `Capital represented by participation rights (Genussrechtskapital)` (~page 61)
- Item 300 — `Fund for general banking risks (Fonds für allgemeine Bankrisiken)` (~page 62)
- Item 310 — `Capital (Eigenkapital)` (~page 62/63)
- Item 320 — `Other liabilities (Sonstige Passiva)` (~page 63)

None of them are combined or merged.

**Item 031 or Item 021 anywhere in the document (HV11 section)?**
NO to both. Neither `Item 031` nor `Item 021` appear anywhere in the document. The HV11 (Assets) section goes from Item 010 (Cash) → Item 020 (Balances with central banks) → Item 061 (Loans to banks), with no 021 or 031 defined as named items.
